nodes.label: Unit 2: Noses / The Little Fir Tree
nodes.description: Poem about noses, story about a fir tree, and grammar on nouns

nodes.label: Noses (Poem)
nodes.description: A fun poem about the different types of noses people and animals have

nodes.label: Types of Noses
nodes.description: Long, short, snub, pointed, flat, turned-up, big, small

nodes.label: Message of the Poem
nodes.description: Every nose is special; be happy with the nose you have

nodes.label: The Little Fir Tree (Story)
nodes.description: A fir tree with needle-like leaves wishes for different leaves

nodes.label: Wish 1: Gold Leaves
nodes.description: The fir tree wished for gold leaves, but a man came and stole them all

nodes.label: Wish 2: Glass Leaves
nodes.description: The fir tree wished for glass leaves, but a strong wind shattered them

nodes.label: Wish 3: Broad Green Leaves
nodes.description: The fir tree wished for broad leaves, but a goat came and ate them all

nodes.label: Moral of the Story
nodes.description: Be happy with what you have; your own qualities are the best for you

nodes.label: Grammar: Nouns
nodes.description: Naming words for people, places, animals, and things

nodes.label: Common Nouns
nodes.description: General names like tree, nose, goat, man, forest, wind

nodes.label: Proper Nouns
nodes.description: Specific names that begin with a capital letter like India, Ganga, Monday

nodes.label: How to Spot Nouns
nodes.description: Ask 'Who?' or 'What?' to find nouns in a sentence

nodes.label: Unit Theme: Be Yourself
nodes.description: Both the poem and the story teach us to appreciate what we have and be ourselves
